finding_results.notes: Pursuit speech (line 231) compressed from a multi-sentence thesis with aphorism ('your blankness is the loudest thing in this square') into three short operational bursts that deliver the same tactical information — blank read is unique, physical description was never enough, she identified the void not the face. Aftermath briefing (line 309, already compressed from snapshot's two-paragraph version in a prior pass) tightened from abstract conclusion ('Quiet travel through towns is finished') to route-specific tactical consequence ('Every town between here and the Crook will have the read before we get there'). Both passages now deliver information in pressured, scene-tied bursts rather than composed rhetoric. Aldric's formal register is preserved through uncontracted forms and report-cadence syntax, but the mini-speech architecture and clean aphorisms are removed.

summary: One finding addressed. Aldric's two composed-diagnosis passages — the pursuit-time thesis about identification mechanics and the aftermath briefing — were revised from polished rhetoric into pressured operational bursts tied to immediate scene events. The pursuit speech lost its aphoristic conclusion and multi-clause thesis structure; the aftermath briefing was tightened to a specific tactical consequence referencing their route. Aldric's formal register is maintained through syntax and diction choices, but the composed mini-speech form is broken into shorter, scene-pressured delivery.
